spec_match.rationale: The fluid typography formula is entirely simulated in JS rather than computed by the browser's CSS engine; the registered @property declarations are never referenced in actual CSS rules.

interactive.rationale: Interactive sliders only drive Javascript-recalculated inline font-size properties, failing to demonstrate native CSS-driven computation.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb promises fluid typography built on CSS custom properties, but the preview is driven purely by JS inline styles.

edge_cases.rationale: Checks for @property support, but the demo itself does not use it for rendering.

references.rationale: Completely missing the mandatory references section and the ChromeStatus entry link.

design_system.rationale: Conforms to spacing and builder-grid styles.

openQuestions.title: Implement native CSS fluid type calculation using Container Queries
openQuestions.detail: Refactor the preview panel to use CSS Container Queries (100cqw) instead of Javascript calculation. The .preview-panel should declare container-type: inline-size and its width should be driven by the simulated viewport slider. The .ramp-text size should be determined in pure CSS using the registered @property custom variables and typed arithmetic: clamp(var(--min), calc(var(--min) + (var(--max) - var(--min)) / (1200 - 320) * (100cqw - 320px)), var(--max)).

openQuestions.title: Add ChromeStatus and Specification Reference Links
openQuestions.detail: Add the mandatory references section containing links to chromestatus.com/feature/4740780497043456 and the CSS Values 4 spec.

summary: A concept page that unfortunately operates as a static code card in disguise. Although it registers @property length properties, the actual preview is computed entirely in JavaScript inline font-size calculations rather than native CSS clamp formulas. It is also missing a references section and the ChromeStatus link.
